turns.content: I've been reading about Bevy and the Entity Component System, but I still don't get how the World actually organizes these components before a system runs.

Think of the World as a giant shared map where all your entities are just coordinates on it. When you add a component like Position or Health, you aren't storing that data in an isolated box; instead, you are placing a marker on that coordinate on the global map. The World is essentially a registry that holds references to these markers so they can be accessed by any system. This design matters because if every entity had its own separate memory region for its components, updating one part of your game would require moving entire blocks of data around in RAM, which is incredibly slow and wasteful. By keeping everything on this single global map called the World, systems only need to read or write specific markers at their exact coordinates, making updates blazing fast.
